title: sports-registry — tenancy hooks
description: The registry module ships ONE tenancy lifecycle hook. Distinct from `listeners.json` (domain-event listeners) — the hook fires at a specific tenancy lifecycle point (erasure) and participates in the tenancy transaction. No per-tenant provisioning hook because the platform-seeded catalog is global (loaded once at boot) — new tenants inherit the platform catalog immediately without any per-tenant seed step.

reference.hierarchy_note: See `hierarchy.md` §12 + `tenancy-columns.md` for the tenant plane the registry module lives on.

hooks.purpose: Runs BEFORE tenancy's FK cascade drops the tenant. Iterates every custom Sport / Discipline / Position row belonging to the tenant + verifies no cross-tenant references remain. Historically impossible (cross-tenant references are refused at write) but the hook catches drift from direct SQL edits. Writes a diagnostic entry to the tenancy erasure report + increments `stackra.sports_registry.orphan_check.total` metric. Does NOT rehome the custom rows to platform (platform-seeded is a distinct scope + shape); the actual DELETE fires via the tenant_id FK CASCADE. Platform-seeded rows (tenant_id NULL) are unaffected.

notes: There is NO per-tenant catalog-seed hook. The platform catalog is global (tenant_id NULL) + is loaded once at platform boot via SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob triggered by the `foundation::ApplicationBooted` listener. New tenants inherit the platform catalog immediately without any per-tenant seed step.
The tenant-side flow when a tenant lands: the tenant's initial GET /api/v1/sports returns the merged catalog (platform-seeded rows are immediately visible; the tenant has zero customs on day one).

Contrast with age-group (which DOES seed per-tenant defaults on TenantProvisioned): age groups have per-tenant custom rules (e.g. some tenants use rolling_from_birthday, others use academic_year), so per-tenant seeded defaults make sense. Sports have platform-wide uniformity so per-tenant seeding would just duplicate platform data.
See `.kiro/steering/hierarchy.md` §12 for the split between bootstrappers (per-request tenant switch) and hooks (lifecycle events).
